mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes equity prices higher over the near term.
failure_condition: Monthly change in RSAFS has negative correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX return over any rolling 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing investors to de-risk and forward returns to turn negative.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, forward 5-day SPX return is positive more than 45% of the time, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, which compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: In months when real rates are negative, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly greater than zero, or the proportion of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where the unemployment rate is below 4%, further declines signal overheating and margin compression from wage pressure, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE < 4%, the correlation between declines in UNRATE and forward 20-day SPX returns is positive (i.e., falling unemployment associated with rising stocks), or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over three months indicates weakening economic activity, which leads to lower corporate profits and negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 2% over 3 months
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls >2% in 3 months, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive more than 45% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in housing starts and a decline in unemployment signals broad-based economic strength and rising consumer confidence, which boosts equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > previous month AND UNRATE < previous month
failure_condition: In months where both conditions hold, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is not above 55%, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of CPI inflation above 3% year-over-year triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: After CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the 20-day SPX return is negative less than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic recovery when payroll employment is growing above 2% year-over-year, continued strong job gains reinforce income growth and consumption, driving positive equity returns over the near term.
failure_condition: In periods when PAYEMS YoY > 2%, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is not positive, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean.
